sections.title: REFERENCES
sections.llm_prompt: Generate references for a deviation form affecting {process_type} on {product_name}. Include relevant QA- procedures (QA-010 Deviation Management, QA-020 Investigation), PR- process procedures, and any batch records. {description}

sections.title: Description of Deviation
sections.llm_prompt: Write a concise, factual description of a deviation in {process_type} of {product_name}. {description}. State what happened, when, where, who observed it, and what conditions applied. Avoid speculation about root cause.

sections.title: Immediate Actions Taken
sections.llm_prompt: List immediate containment actions for a deviation in {process_type} of {product_name}. Include product quarantine, equipment lockout, sample retention, stakeholder notification, and preservation of evidence.

sections.title: Impact Assessment
sections.llm_prompt: Write an initial impact assessment for a deviation in {product_name}. Address: (1) product quality impact, (2) patient safety impact, (3) other batches impacted, (4) regulatory reporting obligations, (5) data integrity implications.

sections.title: Root-Cause Investigation
sections.llm_prompt: Outline a root-cause investigation plan for a deviation. Cover the 5 Whys or Ishikawa (6M) approach, evidence to collect, interviews, document review, and the analytical tests or retests required for {product_name}.

sections.title: Corrective & Preventive Actions (CAPA)
sections.llm_prompt: Generate CAPA items for a deviation in {process_type} of {product_name}. Include both corrective (CA) actions addressing the specific event and preventive (PA) actions addressing the systemic cause.

default_data.text: Disposition decision (Accept / Rework / Reject / Use-As-Is) with justification. Cross-reference analytical results and QA disposition memo.
